findings.issue: Reviewer report paths are absolute (`/Users/syi/src/universal-paywall/work/...`) instead of repo-relative. Round-1 systemic finding (path-format inconsistency in Reviewers) NOT resolved for T2 — T1, T3, T4, T5 all use repo-relative `work/x402-agent-payment/logs/working/...` form.
findings.fix: Replace the three reviewer lines in tasks/2.md (lines 127-129) with repo-relative form: `work/x402-agent-payment/logs/working/task-2/{reviewer}-{round}.json`.

findings.issue: T3 reviewers include `test-reviewer`, but the task produces a one-off Hardhat spike script + JSON artifact, not a tested code module. There are no unit tests, no TDD anchor (correctly absent), and no vitest/hardhat-test work product for a test-reviewer to evaluate. The choice is defensible (the script must be code-quality-reviewed) but `test-reviewer` is an odd fit; consider whether `test-reviewer` adds review value here or should be dropped.
findings.fix: Either (a) drop `test-reviewer` from frontmatter and Reviewers section if no tests will be written; or (b) add an explicit Acceptance Criterion that the script's JSON-shape assertion logic is itself test-like (e.g., self-check selector assertion is a unit-of-test). No blocking issue — flag for human judgement.

T4 grep guard regex refinement — RESOLVED: regex `^[[:space:]]*[^/*[:space:]].*\b(selfdestruct|delegatecall)\b` correctly skips lines beginning with `/` or `*` so NatSpec `@custom:security-invariant no_selfdestruct no_delegatecall` does not false-trigger; also provides AST-fallback recommendation
